content.content:
- content: 'Claim support systems use several different identifiers. An incident

    reference identifies the reported event before all downstream records are

    reconciled. A claim number identifies an insurer claim record. An evidence

    request ID identifies a specific request for materials within a claim.

    A submission endpoint record identifies a destination associated with a

    request. These identifiers should not be treated as interchangeable.

    '

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to File a Claim With Tesla Insurance

  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/insurance/how-to-file-a-claim-with-tesla-insurance

  - source: Tesla Support

  - domain: tesla.com


  ## Content


  # How to File a Claim With Tesla Insurance


  For Tesla Insurance policyholders, the Tesla app is the primary way to start a new
  claim. In an emergency, contact emergency services first, especially when anyone
  is injured.


  ## Filing a New Claim


  To begin a claim in the Tesla app:


  1. Open the Tesla app.

  2. Open the menu.

  3. Select Tesla Insurance.

  4. Select Claims.

  5. Choose Start New Claim.


  Before filing, be ready with the Tesla Insurance policy number, the vehicle VIN,
  the make, model and model year of the insured vehicle, the incident date and location,
  and a short description of what happened.


  After a claim is submitted, Tesla Insurance assigns a claims representative. That
  representative provides the next instructions for any documentation that is required
  for the claim.


  ## Checking an Existing Claim


  For a claim that has already been submitted, contact the Tesla Insurance claims
  representative identified in the communications received after submission. When
  emailing about an existing claim, include the Tesla Insurance claim number, with
  dashes, in the email subject line. The Claims team can also be reached by phone.


  ## Collision Claims


  A collision claim may involve another vehicle, a person, property, or an object.
  Policy terms and exclusions still apply.


  For an incident involving another person, gather available identifying and contact
  information, along with insurance details. When photographing damage, use lighting
  that makes the damage clear.


  After a collision claim is submitted through the Tesla app, follow the detailed
  instructions sent for the claim. Additional questions should be directed to the
  assigned claim representative.


  ## Other Claim Types


  The support article also describes comprehensive claims, glass-only claims, repairs,
  rentals, towing, total-loss handling and deductibles. Those procedures can differ
  from collision-claim handling.


  For glass-only damage, the process begins by filing a glass claim in the Tesla app.
  For damage that is part of a larger collision, use the collision-claim process instead.


  ## If You Need Help Filing


  Tesla recommends using the Tesla app for the most efficient claim-submission experience.
  If help is needed, contact the Tesla Insurance Claims team.

  '
metadata: {}

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Contact Tesla Insurance
- url:\
  \ https://www.tesla.com/support/insurance/contact-tesla-insurance
- source: Tesla\
  \ Support
- domain: tesla.com

## Content

# Contact Tesla Insurance

Tesla\
  \ Insurance provides separate contact methods for general support, new claims and\
  \ existing claims.

## General Support

For general questions or help, contact\
  \ the Tesla Insurance Support team by email. Include the policy number or claim\
  \ number in the subject line when relevant.

Phone support is also available during\
  \ the hours listed by Tesla.

## New Claims

A new Tesla Insurance claim can\
  \ be filed through the Tesla app. Tesla also lists a Claims phone number for assistance\
  \ with filing.

The new-claim process is distinct from handling questions or documentation\
  \ for a claim that already exists.

## Existing Claims

For questions about\
  \ an existing claim, Tesla directs customers to the Claims team.

Available methods\
  \ include:

- Email the Claims team. The claim number, including dashes, must\
  \ be included in the email subject line.
- Call the Tesla Insurance number and\
  \ follow the prompts for the Claims team.
- Mail claim documentation to the Tesla\
  \ Insurance Claims mailing address.

When mailing documentation, include the Tesla\
  \ Insurance claim number so the material can be associated with the existing claim.
\
  
## Claims Mailing Address

Tesla Insurance Claims  
PO BOX 4000  
Draper,\
  \ UT 84020

## Quotes

Questions about new or existing insurance quotes use\
  \ a separate quote-support process. Tesla lists dedicated contact information for\
  \ quote assistance.

The page distinguishes the process for starting a new claim\
  \ from the process for handling an existing claim. Existing-claim communications\
  \ and mailed documents must be tied to the relevant claim number.
"
metadata: {}
title: Contact Tesla Insurance
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/insurance/contact-tesla-insurance

args.question: Has a Tesla Insurance claim already been submitted? If yes, please provide the claim number exactly as shown (including dashes), the claim representative’s documentation-request link or evidence-request ID, and upload/paste the vehicle photos and incident details you want submitted. If no claim exists yet, confirm that you want me to prepare a new-claim submission using the Tesla app route and provide the incident date, location, description, and policy number. Also confirm that you authorize submission of these materials to Tesla Insurance.
args.reason: Tesla’s official guidance separates starting a new claim in the Tesla app from sending documents for an existing claim; existing-claim materials must be tied to a claim number and an authorized claim-specific destination. The photos and incident details are not available to submit yet.
